ocr.text: The following table presents critical data regarding the treatment outcomes of patients diagnosed with various combinations of
therapies, specifically focusing on their Current (mA) levels and subsequent survival metrics. The analysis includes a diverse
cohort ranging in age and sex, categorized under the Age/Sex column, which allows for a comprehensive understanding of
demographic influences on treatment efficacy. Additionally, the table highlights the Status at Starting Point of Treatment,

providing vital context for the initial conditions of each patient before therapy commencement.

In evaluating the impact of
chemotherapy and radiation therapy, the data reflects significant variations in patient responses, as evidenced by the metrics for
Metastasis (mo) and Survival (mo).
Combination  Current (mA)  Combination  Current (mA)  Combination  Current (mA)
A, B  52  B, W  75  B, S  68
A, W  70  A, S  61  W, S  84
A, B, W  74  A, B, S  70  B, W, S  90
A, W, S  85  A, B, W, S  100
Notably, patients who underwent different combinations of therapy, such as those noted with a Current (mA) of 20, experienced
distinct outcomes compared to those with higher Current (mA) levels.
Age/Sex  Grade,  Status at Starting Point of Treatment  Chemotherapy  Radiation Therapy  Metastasis (mo)  Survival (mo)
40/F  G2  Recurrence at 6 mo after R1 resection  Yes  Yes  Bone (2)  D, 22
42/M  G3  Recurrence at 2 mo after R1 resection  Yes  Yes  Lung(14), bone (20)  D, 27
60/F  G3  Partial resection  Yes  Yes  Chest wall (2), brain (18)  D, 19
38/M  G3  Partial resection  No  No  -  D, 8
44/F  U  Partial resection  No  No  Brain (1)  D, 2
48/M  U  Biopsy  No  No  Lung (3)  D, 5
69/F  G3  Biopsy  No  No  -  D, 3
This differentiation is crucial for tailoring individualized treatment plans and improving prognostic accuracy. Overall, the
insights derived from this table underscore the importance of nuanced treatment approaches in enhancing patient survival and
managing recurrence rates effectively, particularly in cases marked by complexities such as metastasis.
